notes: Confirmed: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor only supports Blazor WebAssembly (browser platform). All Blazor view components are decorated with [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")], the csproj restricts SupportedPlatform to "browser" only, and JSModuleInterop explicitly throws NotSupportedException when not running in WASM. No server-side Blazor rendering pipeline (e.g., SignalR-based command marshaling or SKPicture serialization) exists in the codebase.

actionability.reason: Valid feature request with strong community interest (19 reactions, 48 comments). WASM support was delivered via PR #1811, but server-side Blazor rendering remains unimplemented. .NET 8+ Blazor Auto render mode may reduce urgency.

actions.description: Apply feature-request and Blazor views labels
Cross-reference PR #1811 which delivered Blazor WASM support

proposedResponse.body: Thanks for the suggestion! We investigated and can confirm that server-side Blazor rendering support is not currently available in SkiaSharp. **Investigation:** - All Blazor view components (`SKCanvasView`, `SKGLView`) are decorated with `[SupportedOSPlatform("browser")]` - The `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.csproj` restricts `SupportedPlatform` to `"browser"` only - `JSModuleInterop` throws `NotSupportedException` when not running in WASM - No SignalR-based command marshaling or SKPicture serialization pipeline exists The Blazor WebAssembly side was delivered in `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` (PR #1811), but the server-side rendering requested here remains unimplemented. **Workaround:** For server-only scenarios where interactivity isn't required, you can render with SkiaSharp on the server and serve the output as images via `` tags or data URIs. For interactive rendering, consider using Blazor WebAssembly or the Auto render mode available in .NET 8+, which starts with server-side rendering and transitions to WASM once client assets download.

workarounds: Use SkiaSharp on the server to render to SKImage, encode as PNG/JPEG, and serve via img tags or data URIs for non-interactive scenarios
Use Blazor WebAssembly or .NET 8+ Auto render mode for interactive SkiaSharp rendering
